name: Strategic Narrative & Communication Patterns Ontology
alternateName: NARRATIVE-ONT — Strategic Storytelling, Communication Patterns & Cadenced Lifecycle
description: Formalizes strategic communication as a structured, evidence-backed, audience-aware discipline. Defines the five-section Strategic Narrative Arc (Context → Challenge → Choice → Commitment → Cadence) that transforms VSOM-SA analysis outputs into communicable strategy. Includes seven named Communication Patterns (30-Second Answer, Rented Brain, Ars Rhetorica, Fait Accompli, Dramatic Structure, Deconstruction, Scalable Business Machines) with pattern selection logic driven by communication objective, audience profile, and strategic context. The Communication Lifecycle ensures strategy is sustained through cadenced rhythms tied to VSOM review cycles. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication).
oaa:domain: Strategy Communication, Strategic Storytelling, Organisational Communication

oaa:joinPatterns.name: SA-to-Narrative Pipeline
oaa:joinPatterns.description: The golden chain from SA analysis through narrative construction: VSOM strategy is analysed by REASON, synthesised into a direction summary by PORTFOLIO, then structured as a five-section narrative arc by NARRATIVE. This is the SA → SC handoff.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: vsom:VSOMFramework → rsn:AnalysisSynthesis → pfl:StrategicDirectionSummary → nar:narrativeSourcedFromDirection → nar:StrategicNarrative → nar:hasSection → nar:NarrativeSection
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: End-to-end strategy analysis to communication pipeline

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Pattern Selection Traversal
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Agent traverses from communication objective through audience profile and strategic context to select the optimal communication pattern. This is the core SC decision logic.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: nar:CommunicationObjective → nar:PatternSelection [WHERE audience + context match] → nar:selectsPattern → nar:CommunicationPattern
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: AI agent pattern selection for audience-appropriate communication

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Evidence Chain Traversal
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace from any narrative section back through its evidence chain to the original SA analysis artefacts. Ensures every claim in the narrative is machine-verifiable.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: nar:NarrativeSection → nar:hasEvidenceChain → nar:EvidenceChain → nar:chainReferencesEvidence → rsn:EvidenceItem → rsn:AnalysisSynthesis
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Verify narrative claims against SA evidence base

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Communication Lifecycle Cadence
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Maps communication rhythm to VSOM review cycle. Each cadence point generates updated narrative from current SA data.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Sustained strategy communication tied to review cadence

entities.name: StrategicNarrative
entities.description: Container for a complete strategic narrative arc. A StrategicNarrative transforms SA analysis outputs into a structured, evidence-backed story with five sections (Context, Challenge, Choice, Commitment, Cadence). Each narrative is audience-agnostic at this level — audience-specific translations are handled by CASCADE-ONT. The narrative references a VSOM framework and a Portfolio direction summary as its primary inputs.

oaa:properties.name: vsomFrameworkRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:VSOMFramework @id this narrative communicates

oaa:properties.name: directionSummaryRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to pfl:StrategicDirectionSummary @id as primary content source

oaa:properties.name: synthesisRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:AnalysisSynthesis @id providing the reasoning foundation

oaa:properties.name: strategicContext
oaa:properties.description: Brief context statement: why this strategy, why now

oaa:properties.name: coreMessage
oaa:properties.description: The single-sentence strategic message (the '30-second answer')

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Narrative lifecycle status

oaa:properties.name: approvedBy
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id who approved the narrative

oaa:properties.name: currentVersionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:NarrativeVersion @id for current active version

entities.name: NarrativeSection
entities.description: One of five sections in the Strategic Narrative Arc. Each section serves a distinct communicative purpose and draws from specific SA analysis outputs. The five sections form a sequential story: Context (where we are) → Challenge (why we must change) → Choice (what we will do) → Commitment (how we will execute) → Cadence (how we will sustain). Every section carries an evidence chain linking claims to SA analysis.

oaa:properties.name: sectionType
oaa:properties.description: Which arc section this represents

oaa:properties.name: headline
oaa:properties.description: Section headline — the key message for this section

oaa:properties.name: body
oaa:properties.description: Section body text — the full narrative content

oaa:properties.name: sequenceOrder
oaa:properties.description: Position in the arc (1=Context, 2=Challenge, 3=Choice, 4=Commitment, 5=Cadence)

oaa:properties.name: saDataSources
oaa:properties.description: SA ontology entity references providing data for this section

oaa:properties.name: keyEvidence
oaa:properties.description: Summary of the strongest evidence points supporting this section

oaa:properties.name: tradeOffs
oaa:properties.description: Explicit trade-offs or 'what we will NOT do' (primarily for Choice section)

Context.purpose: Where we are — environmental forces, competitive position, organisational state

Challenge.purpose: Why we must change — strategic tension, evidence chain, consequences of inaction

Choice.purpose: What we will do — strategic direction, where we play, how we win, what we won't do

Commitment.purpose: How we will execute — BSC objectives, OKR cascade, investment allocation, accountability

Cadence.purpose: How we will sustain — review rhythm, communication schedule, early warning signals

entities.name: EvidenceChain
entities.description: Ordered sequence of SA analysis references that back a narrative section. Every claim in the strategic narrative must be traceable to evidence. The chain enables AI agents to verify that narrative content accurately reflects the underlying analysis — and flags when narrative and evidence diverge.

oaa:properties.name: sectionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:NarrativeSection @id this chain supports

oaa:properties.name: evidenceRefs
oaa:properties.description: Ordered references to rsn:EvidenceItem @ids or SA entity @ids

oaa:properties.name: chainStrength
oaa:properties.description: Overall strength of the evidence chain

oaa:properties.name: gapDescription
oaa:properties.description: Known gaps in the evidence chain that should be acknowledged in communication

oaa:properties.name: lastVerifiedDate
oaa:properties.description: Last date the chain was verified against current SA data

entities.name: NarrativeVersion
entities.description: A versioned snapshot of a StrategicNarrative tied to a specific strategic review cycle. Each review cycle (annual, quarterly) may produce an updated narrative version. Versions enable diff comparison: what changed in the strategy story between review cycles, and why.

oaa:properties.name: narrativeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:StrategicNarrative @id

oaa:properties.name: versionNumber
oaa:properties.description: Semantic version (e.g. 1.0.0, 1.1.0)

oaa:properties.name: reviewCycleRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:StrategicReviewCycle @id that triggered this version

oaa:properties.name: changesSummary
oaa:properties.description: Summary of what changed from previous version and why

oaa:properties.name: approvedBy
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id

entities.name: CommunicationPattern
entities.description: A named verbal/persuasion pattern for strategic communication delivery. Each pattern defines a structural approach to how strategy is communicated — not what is communicated (that is the narrative) but how it is framed, structured, and delivered. The seven patterns are: 30-Second Answer, Rented Brain, Ars Rhetorica, Fait Accompli, Dramatic Structure, Deconstruction, and Scalable Business Machines. An AI agent selects patterns based on communication objective, audience profile, and strategic context.

oaa:properties.name: patternCode
oaa:properties.description: Machine-readable pattern identifier

oaa:properties.name: structure
oaa:properties.description: Structural pattern: how the communication is organized (e.g. 'Position → Because → Therefore' for 30-Sec Answer)

oaa:properties.name: whenToUse
oaa:properties.description: Guidance on when this pattern is most effective

oaa:properties.name: primaryObjectives
oaa:properties.description: Communication objectives this pattern best serves

oaa:properties.name: bestForAudiences
oaa:properties.description: Audience levels this pattern works best for

oaa:properties.name: saSourceMapping
oaa:properties.description: Which SA entity types provide data for this pattern

RentedBrain.structure: Empathy framing → Shared constraints → Strategic insight → Co-ownership

ArsRhetorica.structure: Ethos (credibility) → Logos (logic) → Pathos (emotion) → Call to action

FaitAccompli.structure: Early wins → Current momentum → Evidence of progress → What's next

DramaticStructure.structure: Exposition → Rising Action → Climax → Falling Action → Resolution

Deconstruction.structure: Decompose complex → Explain each component → Reconstruct the whole

ScalableBusinessMachines.structure: Machine model → Inputs/Processes/Outputs/Metrics per machine → Tuning strategy

entities.name: PatternSelection
entities.description: An AI agent's pattern selection decision record. Captures the reasoning behind choosing a specific CommunicationPattern for a given communication task. The selection is driven by three inputs: communication objective, audience profile (from RRR roles and cascade level), and strategic context (from VSOM review cycle trigger). This entity makes the agent's decision transparent and auditable.

oaa:properties.name: objectiveRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:CommunicationObjective @id

oaa:properties.name: audienceLevel
oaa:properties.description: Target audience level

oaa:properties.name: audienceRoleRef
oaa:properties.description: Specific reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id if applicable

oaa:properties.name: contextTrigger
oaa:properties.description: Strategic context that triggered this communication

oaa:properties.name: selectedPatternRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:CommunicationPattern @id selected

oaa:properties.name: selectionRationale
oaa:properties.description: Why this pattern was selected for this combination of objective, audience, and context

oaa:properties.name: alternativePatterns
oaa:properties.description: Other patterns considered and why they were not selected

oaa:properties.name: selectedBy
oaa:properties.description: Agent or human who made the selection

entities.name: CommunicationObjective
entities.description: The purpose category for a strategic communication. Four objectives define why the communication exists: Inform (share knowledge), Persuade (change minds), Mobilise (drive action), Sustain (maintain alignment over time). Each objective maps to different communication patterns and success metrics.

oaa:properties.name: objectiveType
oaa:properties.description: Communication purpose category

oaa:properties.name: successCriteria
oaa:properties.description: How to measure whether the communication achieved its objective

oaa:properties.name: recommendedPatterns
oaa:properties.description: CommunicationPattern codes best suited to this objective

oaa:properties.name: typicalCadence
oaa:properties.description: Whether this objective typically requires one-time or recurring communication

entities.name: CommunicationLifecycle
entities.description: A cadenced communication rhythm tied to VSOM strategic review cycles. Strategy communication is not a one-time event — it is a sustained rhythm: annual strategy refresh, quarterly strategic review, monthly progress pulse, and event-driven strategic alerts. Each cadence point generates or updates communication artefacts from current SA data. The lifecycle ensures strategy communication evolves alongside the strategy itself.

oaa:properties.name: vsomFrameworkRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:VSOMFramework @id this lifecycle supports

oaa:properties.name: reviewCycleRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:StrategicReviewCycle @id governing the cadence

oaa:properties.name: cadenceType
oaa:properties.description: Communication rhythm frequency

oaa:properties.name: triggerCondition
oaa:properties.description: What triggers this cadence point (e.g. 'Quarterly BSC review complete', 'KPI breach detected')

oaa:properties.name: outputArtefacts
oaa:properties.description: Communication artefacts generated at this cadence point

oaa:properties.name: targetAudiences
oaa:properties.description: Audience levels that receive communication at this cadence

oaa:properties.name: narrativeUpdateScope
oaa:properties.description: How much of the narrative is updated at this cadence point

oaa:properties.name: kpiThresholdRefs
oaa:properties.description: KPI thresholds that trigger event-driven communication

relationships.name: Narrative Aligned To VSOM
relationships.description: A strategic narrative communicates a specific VSOM framework strategy

relationships.name: Narrative Sourced From Direction
relationships.description: Narrative content is sourced from the SA capstone direction summary — the primary SA → SC handoff

relationships.name: Has Section
relationships.description: Strategic narrative contains five narrative sections (exactly one of each type)

relationships.name: Has Evidence Chain
relationships.description: Narrative section has an evidence chain linking claims to SA analysis

relationships.name: Chain References Evidence
relationships.description: Evidence chain references SA evidence items for traceability

relationships.name: Section Evidenced By Synthesis
relationships.description: Narrative section is evidenced by SA reasoning synthesis

relationships.name: Has Version
relationships.description: Strategic narrative has versioned snapshots tied to review cycles

relationships.name: Version Triggered By Review
relationships.description: Narrative version was triggered by a VSOM strategic review cycle

relationships.name: Selects Pattern
relationships.description: Pattern selection decision selects a specific communication pattern

relationships.name: Pattern Achieves Objective
relationships.description: Communication pattern achieves a communication objective

relationships.name: Lifecycle Triggered By Review
relationships.description: Communication lifecycle cadence tied to VSOM review cycle

relationships.name: Lifecycle Updates Narrative
relationships.description: Each lifecycle cadence point generates an updated narrative version

relationships.name: Narrative Approved By Role
relationships.description: Strategic narrative approved by an executive role

businessRules.name: Narrative Must Have Five Sections
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly five NarrativeSection instances: Context, Challenge, Choice, Commitment, Cadence (one of each sectionType)

businessRules.name: Narrative Must Reference VSOM
businessRules.action: THEN vsomFrameworkRef MUST reference a valid vsom:VSOMFramework @id

businessRules.name: Narrative Must Have Direction Source
businessRules.action: THEN directionSummaryRef MUST reference a valid pfl:StrategicDirectionSummary @id

businessRules.name: Evidence Chain Must Have References
businessRules.action: THEN evidenceRefs MUST contain at least one valid SA entity reference

businessRules.name: Pattern Selection Must Have Rationale
businessRules.action: THEN selectionRationale MUST be non-empty, explaining why the pattern was chosen

businessRules.name: Active Narrative Must Have Approved Version
businessRules.action: THEN currentVersionRef MUST reference a NarrativeVersion with status 'Active' or 'Approved'

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release of NARRATIVE-ONT as part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication). Formalizes the Strategic Narrative Arc, 7 Communication Patterns, Pattern Selection logic, and Communication Lifecycle. Cross-references VSOM-ONT v3.0.0, REASON-ONT v1.0.0, PORTFOLIO-ONT v1.0.0, BSC-ONT v1.0.0, MACRO-ONT v1.0.0, RRR-ONT v4.0.0, KPI-ONT v1.0.0.

What is the role and purpose of NarrativeVersion within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of CommunicationPattern within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of PatternSelection within this ontology?
